design_philosophy.core_loop: Match pairs of mahjong tiles by selecting tiles with at least one free vertical edge until the board clears or no legal moves remain
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Spatial constraint solving—identifying which tile pairs can be matched given positional restrictions, leading to eventual deadlock states
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Meditative puzzle mastery; clearing ornate tile layouts and revealing the dragon's eye animation as reward
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Enforces strict matching rules (free edge requirement) that create inherent deadlock risk; rejects arbitrary tile removal and emphasizes foresight and planning over reflexes

difficulty.checkpointing: Single-session play; game ends on deadlock or completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Layout randomization or multiple board configurations provide replay variety; no explicit difficulty selector documented

progression.unlock_mechanism: Completing a board reveals the dragon animation (visual reward progression)
replayability_hooks: Dragon's eye animation reveal on completion

world_building_techniques: Oriental aesthetic via mahjong tile imagery
Dragon's eye reveal as thematic closure

environmental_storytelling: Dragon eye animation as victory emblem
Mahjong tile symbolism (winds, dragons, seasons, flowers) embedded in gameplay

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Shanghai II: Dragon's Eye SNES)
